mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides automated scaling, monitoring, and security features, making it suitable for production workloads. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This command installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and runs the installation script for the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.1 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual compartment and VCN OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.1 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region>

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command verifies that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

```

> This command applies the monitoring configuration.
> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

oci bv volume delete --volume-id <your_volume_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_volume_id> with the ID of any orphaned volumes.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of your nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify all nodes are in 'Ready' state before proceeding.
> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that all nodes are back to 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using the correct cluster ID and region.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user may not have the necessary IAM permissions in Oracle Cloud.

**Fix:**
Ensure the user has the required policies attached in Oracle Cloud IAM.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to network issues or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the node's logs and ensure there are enough resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not provisioning
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the subnets are not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Verify the subnet configurations in your load balancer settings.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved load balancer configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new node shape configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OCI CLI installed and configured before proceeding.
